commands_and_summary:
Call to tool write_to_file with arguments {"filename": "Dockerfile", "text": "FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git gnupg software-properties-common

# Add Ruby PPA repository for Ruby >= 3.0.0
RUN add-apt-repository ppa:brightbox/ruby-ng --yes

# Add Hyperledger repository keys
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61 && \
    apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys CE7709D068DB5E88

# Add Hyperledger repository for dependencies
RUN echo "deb https://hyperledger.jfrog.io/artifactory/indy focal dev" >> /etc/apt/sources.list

# Install system dependencies including Python and required libraries
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq \
    python3 python3-pip python3-nacl rocksdb=5.8.8 \
    libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev \
    zstd ruby3.0 ruby3.0-dev build-essential libssl1.0.0 ursa=0.3.2-1 libindy=1.15.0~1625-bionic

# Install Python build-time requirements
RUN pip3 install -U setuptools==50.3.2 'pyzmq==22.3.0' Cython==0.29.36

# Install the fpm for packaging
RUN gem install --no-document rake dotenv:2.8.1 fpm:1.14.2

# Clean up
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone the project repository
WORKDIR /app
ARG REPO_URL
RUN test -n "$REPO_URL" && git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" indy_node

# Set the working directory to the cloned project
WORKDIR /app/indy_node

# Install project dependencies
RUN pip --cache-dir /root/.cache/pip install .[tests]

# Default command to run on container start
CMD ["/bin/bash"]"}

Important commands: Commands related to repository setup include `add-apt-repository` for adding PPA, and `apt-get update && apt-get install ...` for installing dependencies.

Call to tool write_to_file with arguments {"filename": "Dockerfile", "text": "FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git gnupg software-properties-common

# Introduce Hyperledger repository keys
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61 && apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys CE7709D068DB5E88

# Install dependencies excluding previous failed components
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq \
    python3 python3-pip python3-nacl \
    libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev \
    zstd ruby ruby-dev build-essential python3-dev

# Install Python build-time requirements
RUN pip3 install -U setuptools==50.3.2 'pyzmq==22.3.0' Cython==0.29.36

# Clean up unnecessary files

# Clone the project repository

ARG REPO_URL
RUN test -n "$REPO_URL" && git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" indy_node

# Set the working directory to the cloned project

RUN pip --cache-dir /root/.cache/pip install .[tests]

# Default command to run on container start
CMD ["/bin/bash"]"}

written_files:
FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git gnupg software-properties-common

# Add Ruby PPA repository for Ruby >= 3.0.0
RUN add-apt-repository ppa:brightbox/ruby-ng --yes

# Add Hyperledger repository keys
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61 && \
    apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys CE7709D068DB5E88

# Add Hyperledger repository for dependencies
RUN echo "deb https://hyperledger.jfrog.io/artifactory/indy focal dev" >> /etc/apt/sources.list

# Install system dependencies including Python and required libraries
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq \
    python3 python3-pip python3-nacl rocksdb=5.8.8 \
    libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev \
    zstd ruby3.0 ruby3.0-dev build-essential libssl1.0.0 ursa=0.3.2-1 libindy=1.15.0~1625-bionic

# Install Python build-time requirements
RUN pip3 install -U setuptools==50.3.2 'pyzmq==22.3.0' Cython==0.29.36

# Install the fpm for packaging
RUN gem install --no-document rake dotenv:2.8.1 fpm:1.14.2

# Clean up
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone the project repository
WORKDIR /app
ARG REPO_URL
RUN test -n "$REPO_URL" && git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" indy_node

# Set the working directory to the cloned project
WORKDIR /app/indy_node

# Install project dependencies
RUN pip --cache-dir /root/.cache/pip install .[tests]

# Default command to run on container start
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git gnupg software-properties-common

# Introduce Hyperledger repository keys
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61 && apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys CE7709D068DB5E88

# Install dependencies excluding previous failed components
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq \
    python3 python3-pip python3-nacl \
    libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev \
    zstd ruby ruby-dev build-essential python3-dev

# Install Python build-time requirements
RUN pip3 install -U setuptools==50.3.2 'pyzmq==22.3.0' Cython==0.29.36

# Clean up unnecessary files
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone the project repository
WORKDIR /app
ARG REPO_URL
RUN test -n "$REPO_URL" && git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" indy_node

# Set the working directory to the cloned project
WORKDIR /app/indy_node

# Install project dependencies, altered for testing purposes
RUN pip --cache-dir /root/.cache/pip install .[tests]

# Default command to run on container start
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

previous_attempts.problems: The Docker build for the image failed multiple times due to various reasons. Key issues included: (1) Missing packages such as 'rocksdb', 'libindy', and 'libssl1.0.0' during installation attempts. (2) Incorrect Ruby version (2.5.0) that does not satisfy the requirements for the 'fpm' packaging gem which requires Ruby >= 3.0.0. (3) Missing ZMQ development libraries causing failures when installing 'pyzmq' and 'Cython'. (4) The 'gem' command not found, indicating Ruby was not properly installed. (5) Errors related to repository verification and package installations, particularly with Sovrin repositories. The agent consistently encountered issues during steps 6-20, failing to build the Docker image due to the aforementioned reasons.
previous_attempts.actions: The initial approach involved creating a Dockerfile based on 'ubuntu:18.04' to install required dependencies. As issues arose, the Dockerfile was modified to include additional repositories, change Ruby versions, and install various libraries. Specific steps included: (1) Installing 'git' and 'gnupg' first. (2) Adding Hyperledger repository keys. (3) Attempting to install system dependencies including Python, Ruby, and various libraries. (4) Several iterations of modifying the Dockerfile to correct missing dependencies or adjust versions. (5) Addressing issues with package installations by attempting to install specific versions or additional development libraries. (6) Encountering and attempting to resolve issues with missing commands, such as 'gem'.
previous_attempts.progress: 30% complete. The Docker image was not successfully built, with multiple failures at various steps. Key dependencies were not installed correctly, and the environment is not fully functional. Docker container is not running due to build failures.
previous_attempts.lessons: The project requires specific versions of dependencies, particularly Ruby (>= 3.0.0) for certain gems. The installation of ZMQ development libraries is necessary for the 'pyzmq' package. The Dockerfile must ensure that all required repositories are added early in the build process to avoid failures related to missing packages. Additionally, a C compiler is essential for building Python packages that include C extensions.
previous_attempts.suggestions: 1. Modify the Dockerfile to ensure Ruby 3.0.0 is installed from a valid PPA. 2. Add installation of 'libzmq3-dev' to ensure 'pyzmq' can compile. 3. Ensure that 'build-essential' is included to provide the necessary build tools. 4. Before running 'gem install', confirm that Ruby is correctly installed and accessible. 5. Avoid using deprecated or non-existent repositories that can cause installation failures.
previous_attempts.dockerfile_recommendation:
FROM ubuntu:18.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y software-properties-common git gnupg

# Add required repositories for Ruby
RUN add-apt-repository ppa:brightbox/ruby-ng --yes

# Install dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq \
    python3 python3-pip python3-nacl python3-dev \
    libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev \
    zstd ruby3.0 ruby3.0-dev build-essential libzmq3-dev

# Install Ruby gems needed for the project
RUN gem install --no-document rake dotenv:2.8.1 fpm:1.14.2

# Install setuptools and other Python packages
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir packaging setuptools==50.3.2 'pyzmq==22.3.0' Cython==0.29.36

# Clean apt caches
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone project
WORKDIR /app
ARG REPO_URL
RUN test -n "$REPO_URL" && git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" indy_node

# Set working directory to the project
WORKDIR /app/indy_node

# Install project dependencies
RUN pip --cache-dir /root/.cache/pip install .[tests]

CMD ["/bin/bash"]
previous_attempts.dockerfile_used:
FROM ubuntu:18.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git and gnupg separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git gnupg

# Add Hyperledger repository keys
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61

# Add Ruby PPA
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y software-properties-common && \
    add-apt-repository ppa:brightbox/ruby-ng --yes

# Install Ruby and related tools before other dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq \
    python3 python3-pip python3-nacl python3-dev \
    libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev \
    build-essential zstd ruby ruby-dev

# Install Ruby gems needed for the project
RUN gem install --no-document rake dotenv:2.8.1 fpm:1.14.2

# Installing packaging module
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir packaging

# Install setuptools first
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir -U setuptools==50.3.2

# Install other Python packages
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir -U 'pyzmq==22.3.0' Cython==0.29.36

# Move ursa libs if the directory exists.
RUN if [ -d /usr/lib/ursa ]; then mv /usr/lib/ursa/* /usr/lib && rm -rf /usr/lib/ursa; fi

# Clean apt caches
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone project
WORKDIR /app
ARG REPO_URL
RUN test -n "$REPO_URL" && git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" indy_node

# Set working directory to the project
WORKDIR /app/indy_node

# Install project dependencies
RUN pip --cache-dir /root/.cache/pip install .[tests]

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

previous_attempts.problems: Multiple build failures occurred while attempting to build the Docker image for the project due to missing dependencies and version conflicts. Specifically, errors were encountered with the installation of packages such as 'rocksdb', 'libindy', 'ursa', and 'ruby3.0'. The build at step 14 failed with the error that 'fpm' requires Ruby version >= 3.0.0, while the installed version was 2.7.0. Step 20 failed due to a Ruby version conflict during the installation of gems, and step 15 faced issues with missing packages 'ruby3.0' and 'ruby3.0-dev'. The root cause of these failures includes incorrect package names, dependencies missing from the repositories, and incompatible Ruby versions.
previous_attempts.actions: 1. Initially, a Dockerfile was created using Ubuntu 20.04 as the base image. 2. Essential packages including 'gnupg' and 'git' were installed first. 3. Hyperledger repository keys were added, followed by attempts to install several required packages. 4. After encountering failures, the Dockerfile was modified to include different repository sources and package versions, but issues persisted, especially with 'sovrin' repositories. 5. The version of Ruby was adjusted in the Dockerfile, but conflicts persisted regarding the required version for installing Ruby gems. 6. Multiple iterations of the Dockerfile were created to resolve dependency issues, including commenting out failing repositories and changing the Ruby version.
previous_attempts.progress: Approximately 30% complete. The Docker build process has not successfully completed due to repeated failures during package installation. The environment setup is mostly attempted, but key dependencies are missing, and no successful test execution has occurred.
previous_attempts.lessons: The project has specific dependencies that must be carefully managed. Key dependencies include 'rocksdb', 'libindy', and 'fpm', which requires Ruby >= 3.0.0. The repositories used for package installations must be verified for availability. Additionally, the version constraints on Ruby and its gems need to be strictly adhered to avoid conflicts. The installation commands for some packages may need to be validated against the current Ubuntu version repositories.
previous_attempts.suggestions: 1. Change the base image to 'ubuntu:20.04' and ensure it is up to date. 2. Install Ruby 3.0 or higher explicitly by using the 'brightbox' PPA. 3. Use the command 'apt-get install -y ruby3.0 ruby3.0-dev' to ensure the correct Ruby version is available. 4. Make sure to validate the availability of all required packages before running the build, especially 'rocksdb', 'libindy', and 'ursa'. 5. If certain repositories are causing issues, consider removing or replacing them with alternative sources.
previous_attempts.dockerfile_recommendation:
FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Update package lists and install essential packages including GnuPG and Ruby 3.0 first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gnupg git software-properties-common

# Add Ruby PPA repository
RUN add-apt-repository ppa:brightbox/ruby-ng --yes

# Add Hyperledger repository keys
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61 && \
    apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys CE7709D068DB5E88

# Add Hyperledger repository
RUN echo "deb https://hyperledger.jfrog.io/artifactory/indy focal dev" >> /etc/apt/sources.list

# Install other system dependencies with specific Ruby version
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq \
    python3 python3-pip python3-nacl \
    ruby3.0 ruby3.0-dev zlib1g-dev

# Install Python packages
RUN pip3 install -U setuptools==50.3.2 'pyzmq==22.3.0' Cython==0.29.36

# Install fpm gem without documentation
RUN gem install --no-document rake dotenv:2.8.1 fpm:1.14.2

# Clean up unnecessary files
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone project
WORKDIR /app
ARG REPO_URL
RUN test -n "$REPO_URL" && git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" indy_node

# Set working directory to the project
WORKDIR /app/indy_node

# Install runtime dependencies
RUN pip --cache-dir /root/.cache/pip install .[tests]
